_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
H2Accelerate TRUCKS is a pre-commercial funded pilot, not a regulatory compliance obligation. The workspace — operating as a freight forwarder booking road carrier capacity for European intra-EU movements across live events, fine art, luxury goods, film and TV, high-value automotive, and humanitarian verticals — sits downstream of this initiative rather than within it. The workspace's position in the supply chain is that of a transport buyer, not a vehicle operator, fleet owner, OEM customer, or energy infrastructure developer.

The direct participants bearing obligations and commitments under the H2Accelerate TRUCKS grant agreement (No. 101101446) are: (i) the OEM vehicle manufacturers — Volvo Group (anchor), Scania, Hyundai Hydrogen Mobility Germany, and retrofitter Hyliko — as vehicle suppliers; (ii) over 20 selected European truck operators as the OEM's trusted customers deploying and operating the trucks; and (iii) hydrogen infrastructure partners — Everfuel, TotalEnergies, and TEAL Mobility — as refuelling station providers. The workspace is not named or contemplated as a participant in any of these tiers.

The workspace's compliance-chain relevance is indirect and arises from two regulatory instruments that are separate from, but causally related to, the H2Accelerate initiative: (a) Regulation (EU) 2024/1610 amending the HDV CO2 standards, which will impose binding fleet-average emission reduction requirements on road carriers from 2030 and creates structural pressure on those carriers to adopt zero-emission vehicles; and (b) AFIR (Regulation (EU) 2023/1804), which governs the mandatory deployment of hydrogen refuelling infrastructure on TEN-T core corridors by 31 December 2030. The workspace's road carrier partners — not the workspace itself — are the regulated entities under the HDV CO2 standards. However, if those carriers internalise hydrogen fuel costs into freight rates or surcharges, and if clients with CSRD sustainability reporting obligations begin requesting carrier-level GHG intensity data, the workspace will face commercial and reporting questions that trace back to this infrastructure and vehicle deployment ecosystem.

*Legal Confirmation Required: the workspace should confirm with legal counsel that its status as a freight forwarder/transport buyer does not create any secondary registration, reporting, or due-diligence obligation under the HDV CO2 standards framework or under any implementing measures expected post-2026.*

*Source: CORDIS Project Fact Sheet, H2Accelerate TRUCKS, Grant Agreement 101101446, European Commission. https://cordis.europa.eu/project/id/101101446. Clean Hydrogen Partnership Project Dashboard, H2Accelerate TRUCKS. https://www.clean-hydrogen.europa.eu/projects-dashboard/projects-repository/h2accelerate-trucks_en. Regulation (EU) 2024/1610, EUR-Lex OJ:L_202401610. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=OJ:L_202401610. Regulation (EU) 2023/1804 (AFIR), EUR-Lex CELEX:32023R1804. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX:32023R1804.*
